challenge.name: Smart Demand Signals
challenge.full_title: Smart Demand Signals: Predicción de necesidad de compra y detección temprana de riesgo de abandono en clínicas dentales

strategic_framing.alignment: Combines specialisation, innovation, and sustainable growth — Inibsa's positioning
strategic_framing.sustainability_link: Better commercial execution = more efficient use of time, effort, resources; optimised travel/displacement; 'generate more with less work'
strategic_framing.barcelona_link: Barcelona as the base of an industrial and health-sector project with international vocation

context.company: Inibsa is a locally-based pharmaceutical manufacturing company committed to European standards and sustainability criteria (efficient water and energy use).

scale.client_base: ~7,000 dental clinics (challenge dataset focuses on ~6,000 with recurring commercial activity)
scale.data_history: >5 years of sales at client/product/date granularity
scale.geographic_focus: Spain (data more reliable than Portugal; Portugal out of initial scope)

context.business_problem: Transform purchasing patterns into actionable predictive signals to promote sales of locally produced products and improve supply chain anticipation.
context.core_pipeline: Purchase history → Signal detection → Alert → Commercial action

session_objectives: Clarify the real environment of the problem
Delimit the scope of the dataset
Align expectations on the expected solution

scope_focus: Product families that concentrate most of sales
Individual clinics with recurring commercial activity

client_segments.loyal: Concentrate most of their demand with Inibsa
client_segments.promiscuous: Split demand with competitors or buy via alternative channels (depósitos)
client_segments.marginal: Buy residually / zero or marginal purchases

commodity_products.key_constraint: Competitor activity is NOT directly observable — must be inferred from observed purchase pattern and client's relative potential.
solution_must: Detect uncaptured demand in promiscuous clients
Estimate the optimal moment of contact
Identify early signals of deterioration in stable clients
modeling_requirements: Expected consumption models by clinic typology
Identification of structurally promiscuous clients (vs. one-off divergence)

technical_products.variability_source: Depends on type of clinical case, professional specialty, competitor penetration
technical_products.interpretation_risk: Confusing a normal pause with real churn
technical_products.what_can_happen: Some clients show enough recurrence to detect deviations — but that regularity is NOT guaranteed and must not be read rigidly.
technical_products.recurrence_note: Recurrence varies by clinic profile and product use type (systematic vs. occasional). Solution should incorporate, when it adds value, differences by clinic profile, specialty, and individual historical behaviour.
technical_products.must_distinguish: Sporadic patterns compatible with normal product use vs. real commercial deterioration

minimum_questions_to_answer: Anticipate when a client should buy a product family again
Estimate when there is a real capture window vs. competitor
Detect when an apparently stable client starts showing churn signals
Distinguish normal consumption variations from relevant abandonment signals
Transform signals into useful, explainable, actionable commercial alerts
objective: Design an analytical solution that identifies, on a daily basis, signals indicating the need for commercial intervention at the level of client × product family × timing — differentiating commodity vs. technical products.

not_useful: An alert/classification the commercial team cannot interpret

optimal_contact_moment.definition: Not the same across cases — solution must include intervention logic (static client classification is NOT enough).

by_segment.loyal_clients: When expected purchase frequency approaches or stock seems near depletion
by_segment.promiscuous_clients: When there is a reasonable window for capture against competitor
by_segment.churn_risk_clients: When historical pattern shows relevant deterioration vs. expected

functional_requirements.actionable_decision_unit: client × product family × reason × recommended intervention moment
functional_requirements.differentiated_logic_by_category: No universal logic expected — commodity vs. technical must be treated differently
functional_requirements.architecture: Standalone first, CRM-agnostic, designed for future integration
functional_requirements.deployment_phase: Standalone deploy is in scope; CRM integration is phase 2 (out of scope for the challenge but must be contemplated from the start)
functional_requirements.geographic_scalability: Initial focus on Spain; designed for future extension to other countries
functional_requirements.post_alert_lifecycle: Must define who manages the alert, within what timeframe, and how the result is recorded — to avoid the system becoming an analytical layer with no real execution impact
functional_requirements.feedback_loop: Record alert + commercial action + outcome to improve the model and detect false positives over time

open_approach.framing: Open innovation problem — teams choose business rules, statistical models, ML, or hybrid approaches.
valued_if_added: Behaviour differences by clinic segment (using available data)
Specific treatment of sporadic-but-valid clients in technical products
Deviation detection vs. individual historical pattern
Incorporation of external/complementary variables, if analytically justified

minimum_architecture.alert_exposure: Path that facilitates future CRM integration

expected_business_impact: Increase order frequency in accounts with uncaptured demand
Reduce client loss / sales deterioration in loyal clients
Improve commercial reaction capacity to early signals
system_success_metrics: Progressive model performance improvement over time

contextual: Clientes: client data with location, type
Potencial: purchase potential per client (and family)

data_format: Client and product IDs are anonymized
Sales values are in fictional monetary units (can be negative — returns)
Potential is provided per client and family, in the same units as sales
data_quality_concerns: Anomalies: extraordinary orders, promotions, stock breaks, commercial policy changes
interpretive_caveats: Units may be delivered at zero cost (replacement of damaged product)
Apparent lack of recurrence may be because the client is not registered or is delegated to a representative — not real absence
Client potential may be lower than real sold units due to mis-reported data
Campaigns may distort the pattern (volume noise observed as peaks rather than demonstrated structural demand)

expected_deliverables: Early churn-risk detection for technical products

interpretation: Assuming competitor or indirect-channel purchases are observable — they are not
Treating every purchase silence as churn
solution_design: Producing alerts without explanation or prioritization
expectation: Aiming for a finished, closed implementation rather than a plausible, defensible solution

what_a_good_proposal_does: Differentiates well between commodities and technical products
Is operational in a real commercial environment
Has plausible evolution toward real use
Note: the key is not technical sophistication but analytical and commercial utility

do_not_expect: Integration with CRM or other systems
A solution tied to a specific technology

evaluation_criteria: Translation of complex business problem into useful analytical solution
Prioritisation and fit within commercial process
Scalability and viability of future evolution
task_for_assistant: Help design and prototype the Smart Demand Signals system. When proposing approaches, explicitly: (1) distinguish commodity vs. technical product logic; (2) for commodities — model expected demand vs. potential and detect uncaptured demand without observing competitors directly; (3) for technical products — distinguish normal variability, temporal displacement, and real deterioration; (4) generate alerts with the minimum payload (contact notice, reason, product family, urgency), full traceability, and prioritisation by economic impact / conversion probability / time urgency; (5) define an optimal-contact-moment logic per segment (loyal / promiscuous / churn-risk) — not a static classification; (6) propose a standalone, CRM-agnostic architecture with a feedback loop on alert outcomes; (7) flag data-quality assumptions and interpretive caveats explicitly.
